name: Ant.Alert
comment:
Alert component

**This component has a stateless and a stateful API**

<https://ant.design/components/alert/>

Alerts inform your users of relevant information.

@docs Alert, AlertType


## Stateless API

The following functions allow you to simply plop in an alert that never emits `Msg`s. If you want to control their visibility, you would have to do so on your own.

@docs alert
@docs withType
@docs withDescription
@docs toHtml


## Stateful API

If you want to render interactive alerts, you will have to create a "stack" of stateful alerts, which can contain between 0 and `n` `Alert`s. Every alert inside this stack is interactive - the user can close them at will. In other words, the alerts in this stack produce `Msg` values that you must handle.

There are 3 things you must do in order to properly manage your stateful stack of alerts:

1.  initialize the stack with `initAlertStack` and save this stack in your model.
2.  Create a `Msg` value that contains a `Alert.Msg`
3.  Handle `Alert.Msg` values with updateAlertStack

A alert stack is nothing more than just a `List (Alert msg)` (with some internal mutations done to them), so to show them in your view, all you need to do is call `List.map toHtml statefulAlerts`

Example:

    type Msg
        = AlertMsg Alert.Msg

    model : Model
    model =
        { statefulAlertStack =
            initAlertStack AlertMsg
                [ alert "Hey there, you can close me :)"
                ]
        }

    update : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    update msg model =
        case msg of
            AlertMsg alertMsg ->
                let
                    ( alertModel, alertCmd ) =
                        updateAlertStack AlertMsg alertMsg model.statefulAlertStack
                in
                ( { closeableAlerts = alertModel }
                , alertCmd
                )

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view { statefulAlertStack } =
        div [] <|
            stackToHtml statefulAlertStack

@docs Msg, CloseableAlertStack

@docs initAlertStack, updateAlertStack, stackToHtml

unions.name: Alert
unions.comment:
An `Alert` is what is returned when you create an alert component

You can customize alerts.

To turn an `Alert` into `Html msg` call `toHtml`

unions.name: AlertType
unions.comment: The kind of alert. Determines the colors used for background and border.

unions.name: CloseableAlertStack
unions.comment: Represents the state of closeable alerts

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Messages emitted by closeable alerts

values.name: initAlertStack
values.comment:
THIS API IS UNSTABLE AND ALSO VISUALLY UNAPPEALING. DO NOT USE STATEFUL ALERTS YET.

Takes a set of stateless alerts that don't ever emit messages and turns them into Alerts that a user can interact with (i.e. close them).

See the example above to see the usage of `initAlertStack`.

This alert stack must be saved in your model in order for it's state to be tracked and altered.

Every time a user clicks on the close icon, the alert will emit a `msg`.

values.name: stackToHtml
values.comment: Render a stack of closeable alerts into your `view`.

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Convert an `Alert` into a `Html msg`

    alert "Hellow, world!"
        |> toHtml

values.name: updateAlertStack
values.comment: Update a stack of closeable alerts. This function should be hooked up to your `update` function.
values.type: (Ant.Alert.Msg -> msg) -> Ant.Alert.Msg -> Ant.Alert.CloseableAlertStack msg -> ( Ant.Alert.CloseableAlertStack msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: withDescription
values.comment:
Provide a descriptive label / header to your alert, which will be rendered in bigger / emphasized text.

    alert "Very long alert that may require a more succinct label / summary"
        |> withDescription "Invalid Password"
        |> toHtml

values.name: withType
values.comment:
Change the type of the alert. By default, alerts are Success types.

    alert "Error! Something went wrong!"
        |> withType Error
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Button

unions.name: Button
unions.comment: Represents a button component

unions.name: ButtonSize
unions.comment: Determines the size of the button

unions.name: ButtonType
unions.comment: The type of the button

values.name: button
values.comment:
Create a Button component.

    button "Click Me!"
        |> toHtml

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Make the button disabled. If you have a `onClick` event registered, it will not be fired.

    button "You can't click this"
        |> onClick Logout
        |> disabled True
        |> toHtml

values.name: onClick
values.comment:
Make your button emit messages. By default, clicking a button does nothing.

    button "submit"
        |> onClick FinalCheckoutFormSubmitted
        |> toHtml

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Turn your Button into Html msg

values.name: withIcon
values.comment:
Add an icon to the button

    button "Search"
        |> withIcon searchOutlined
        |> toHtml
values.type: Ant.Icons.Icon msg -> Ant.Button.Button msg -> Ant.Button.Button msg

values.name: withType
values.comment:
Change the default type of the Button

    button "submit"
        |> withType Dashed
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Css
comment:
Global styles that are responsible for the visualas / aesthetic of the elm-antd components. You **must** call `defaultStyles` or `createThemedStyles` at the root of your Elm project.

@docs createThemedStyles, defaultStyles

values.name: createThemedStyles
values.comment:
Creates a global stylesheet based on a custom Theme. See Ant.Theme documentation for more info on `Theme` and creating themes.

You must call `createThemedStyles` at the root of your Elm project!

    div []
        [ Ant.Css.createThemedStyles theme

        -- This button will now be themed according to the primaryColor color you chose!
        , Btn.toHtml <| button "Hello, elm-antd!"
        ]

values.name: defaultStyles
values.comment:
The default stylesheet that adheres to the Antd specification.

You must call `defaultStyles` at the root of your Elm project!

    div []
        [ Ant.Css.defaultTheme

        -- This button will now be themed according to the primaryColor color you chose!
        , Btn.toHtml <| button "Hello, elm-antd!"
        ]

name: Ant.Divider

unions.name: Divider
unions.comment: A customizeable divider that is used to separate pieces of content.

unions.name: Line
unions.comment: The visual look of the divider

unions.name: Orientation
unions.comment: Orientation defines the positioning of the divider label.

unions.name: TextStyle
unions.comment: Defines the size and emphasis of the divider label.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment: Whether the divider is separating content vertically or horizontally.

values.name: divider
values.comment:
Create a Divider component.

    Divider.divider
        |> Divider.toHtml

values.name: withLine
values.comment: Defines the line type of the divider.

values.name: withOrientation
values.comment: Defines the positioning of the label. If your divider doesn't have a label, then this option is redundant.

values.name: withTextStyle
values.comment: Defines whether the divider titles will get rendered as Headings or as smaller text.

values.name: withType
values.comment:
Change the type of the divider. By default the type is `Horizontal`

    verticalDivider =
        Divider.divider
            |> withType Vertical
            |> toHtml

name: Ant.Icons

unions.name: Icon
unions.comment:
An opaque representation of an Icon.

This is the return type of all icon components.

To actually render them onto the dom, you need to call `toHtml`

unions.name: IconSize
unions.comment: The size of the icon. To be used with `withSize`.

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert an `Icon msg` into a `Html msg`

values.name: withDimensions
values.comment:
Alter the width and height of the svg Icon (which also alters the aspect ratio). The width and height passed into this function are then used as the `viewBox` attributes for width and height.

See <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Attribute/viewBox> for more info.

    codeOpenBrackets
        |> withDimensions 100 100
        |> toHtml
values.type: Ant.Icons.Width -> Ant.Icons.Height -> Ant.Icons.Icon msg -> Ant.Icons.Icon msg

values.name: withSize
values.comment:
Alter the size of the Icon.

    codeOpenBrackets
        |> withSize Large
        |> toHtml

values.name: withStyles
values.comment:
Add styles to your SVG Icon

    codeOpenBrackets
        |> withStyles [ ( "width", "100%" ) ]
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Input
comment:
Input widget for data entry

@docs input, InputSize, withSize, onInput, withPlaceholder, toHtml

unions.name: InputSize
unions.comment: Determines the vertical height of the input

values.name: onInput
values.comment: Emit a `msg` event that contains the current value (of type `String`) of the input on every keystroke

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert the input into a `Html msg`

values.name: withPlaceholder
values.comment: Add a placeholder to the input

values.name: withSize
values.comment: Change the size of the input

name: Ant.Layout
comment:
Primitives for creating typical page layouts

From: <https://ant.design/components/layout/>


# Introduction

Each of the primitives below creates a `LayoutTree` that represents that shape of your layout.

This module allows for 2-column and 3-column layouts.

This module defines a recursive LayoutTree data structure, which allows for layouts of large depth and complexity.

@docs LayoutTree


## Primitives

The following primitives take some content (as `Html msg`) and returns to you a `LayoutTree` that you can continue appending to

Example:

    sidebar =
        Layout.sidebar (componentMenu model.activeRoute)
            |> Layout.sidebarWidth 300
            |> Layout.sidebarToTree

    layout : LayoutTree Msg
    layout =
        Layout.layout2
            (Layout.header <| toUnstyled navBar)
            (Layout.layout2
                sidebar
                (Layout.layout2
                    (Layout.content <| toUnstyled componentPageShell)
                    (Layout.footer <| toUnstyled footer)
                )
            )

@docs header, content, footer, layout, layout2


## Customizeable Primitives

The following primitives do not return a `LayoutTree` immediately. Rather, they return an intermediary data structure that you can then customize further before converting it into a `LayoutTree` node that can be appended to your main `LayoutTree`

@docs Sidebar, sidebar, sidebarWidth, sidebarToTree


## Other

@docs toHtml

unions.name: LayoutTree
unions.comment: The various kinds of nodes represented by a LayoutTree

unions.name: Sidebar
unions.comment: A sidebar

values.name: content
values.comment:
Create a content node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered beneath a header (if a header is provided), but above the footer (if a footer is provided)

values.name: footer
values.comment:
Create a footer node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered beneath all other nodes in your LayoutTree, just as you would expect from a footer.

values.name: header
values.comment:
Create a header node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered horizontally at the top of your layout tree

values.name: layout
values.comment: Generate a three-column layout from three LayoutTree's (see example above)
values.type: Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg

values.name: layout2
values.comment: Generate a two-column layout from two LayoutTree's (see example above)
values.type: Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg

values.name: sidebar
values.comment:
Create a customizeable sidebar

In order to append the sidebar to your LayoutTree, you must call `sidebarToTree`

    sideBar innerNodes
        |> sidebarWidth 40
        |> sidebarToTree

values.name: sidebarToTree
values.comment: Convert a `Sidebar` into a `LayoutTree` node so that you may append it to a `LayoutTree`

values.name: sidebarWidth
values.comment: Customize the width (in pixels) of the sidebar

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert your layout tree into good old `Html msg`

name: Ant.Menu
comment:
Primitives for creating Menus.

This module comes with three big menu primitives:

  - Menu: The main container of your menu
  - SubMenu: A menu within your menu. SubMenu's are collapsable.
  - ItemGroup: A grouping of items with a label.

The overarching idea in this module is that you start with the inner items of your menu, constructing it piece by piece, until you have
all of the elements of the Menu. Once that is done, then you can create your Menu and turn it into `Html msg`.

A good example can be found in this project's [showcase](https://github.com/supermacro/elm-antd/blob/master/showcase/src/elm/Router.elm#L277).

@docs initMenuItem, selected, initMenu, initSubMenu, initItemGroup, pushItem, pushSubMenu, pushItemGroup, pushItemToSubMenu, pushItemGroupToSubMenu, pushSubMenuToSubMenu, pushItemToItemGroup, toHtml, Menu, SubMenu, ItemGroup, MenuMode

unions.name: ItemGroup
unions.comment: Represents a grouping of MenuItems

unions.name: Menu
unions.comment: Represents a Menu, its configuration and the Menu's content

unions.name: MenuMode
unions.comment: This type defines how the menu will be positioned

unions.name: SubMenu
unions.comment: Represents a sub-menu, and the submenu's associated configuration / state, as well as the menu's contents

values.name: initItemGroup
values.comment: Initialize a item group with a set of associated MenuItem's

values.name: initMenu
values.comment: Initialize a menu with no content. This is useful when constructing your menu using folds.

values.name: initMenuItem
values.comment: Given a `msg` and some contents, create a menu item

values.name: pushItem
values.comment: push a menu item to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushItemGroup
values.comment: push a item group to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushItemGroupToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a Item Group to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: pushItemToItemGroup
values.comment: Add a MenuItem to the end of the ItemGroup
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg

values.name: pushItemToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a MenuItem to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: pushSubMenu
values.comment: push a SubMenu to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushSubMenuToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a SubMenu to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: selected
values.comment: Mark the menu item as selected

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Turn your Menu into a `Html msg`

name: Ant.Space

unions.name: SpaceDirection
unions.comment: Direction of the layout (think flexbox direction)

unions.name: SpaceSize
unions.comment: The size of the space between elements

values.name: direction
values.comment:
Set the direction of your Space value\\

    space myElementList
        |> direction Horizontal

values.name: space
values.comment:
Create a Space value with default values

    space [ myEl, myOtherEl, mySuperCoolEl ]

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert your Space into a `Html msg`

values.name: withSize
values.comment:
Alter the sizing between elements. By default the size is `Small`

    space elements
        |> withSize Large

name: Ant.Theme

aliases.name: Theme
aliases.comment: elm-antd theme info used to generate custom themes

values.name: createTheme
values.comment:
Create a theme based on a given Primary color. elm-antd takes care of generating the various shades of that color necessary. Note that you need to install `avh4/elm-color` in order to create a `Color` value.

    import Ant.Button as Btn exposing (button)
    import Ant.Css
    import Ant.Theme exposing (createTheme)
    import Color
    import Html exposing (Html, div)

    view : Html msg
    view =
        let
            myThemePrimaryColor =
                Color.rgb255 18 147 216

            theme =
                createTheme myThemePrimaryColor
        in
        div []
            [ Ant.Css.createThemedStyles theme

            -- This button will now be themed according to the primaryColor color you chose!
            , Btn.toHtml <| button "Hello, elm-antd!"
            ]

values.name: defaultTheme
values.comment: The default antd theme. You shouldn't ever use the function since all components already use it under the hood. It is currently exposed as I transition other components over to the themable API. This function **will** be removed / hidden in the future.

name: Ant.Tooltip
comment:
Tooltips for your enjoyment!

@docs tooltip, Tooltip, toHtml, withPosition, TooltipPosition

unions.name: Tooltip
unions.comment: Represents a customizeable tooltip

unions.name: TooltipPosition
unions.comment: Defines the positioning of the Tooltip

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert the Tooltip into a `Html msg`

values.name: tooltip
values.comment:
Create a Tooltip

    tooltip "helpful message"
        |> toHtml

values.name: withPosition
values.comment:
Change the default positioning of the tooltip

    tooltip "on the right"
        |> withPosition Right
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Typography
comment:
Typography components for Elm Antd

@docs title, Level, level, toHtml

unions.name: Level
unions.comment: Level of your headings

values.name: level
values.comment:
Change the heading level of your `Title` component

    title "Elm is cool"
        |> level H3
        |> toHtml

values.name: title
values.comment:
Create a `Title` / header component

By default, the `Title` produced is a `H1`

values.name: paragraph
values.comment: Evenly space out a set of children

name: Ant.Typography.Text
comment:
Create decorated text values

@docs TextType, Text, text, code, keyboard, textType, strong, disabled, underlined, lineThrough, highlighted, toHtml, listToHtml

unions.name: Text
unions.comment: A text value

unions.name: TextType
unions.comment: What kind of text is it?

values.name: code
values.comment:
Turn your Text value into a styled in-line code block text "

I LOVE CATS

" |> code |> toHtml

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Set your Text element as disabled (the cursor icon changes)

    text "can't touch this'"
        |> disabled True
        |> toHtml

values.name: highlighted
values.comment:
Style the Text as highlighted

    text "super important"
        |> highlighted True
        |> toHtml

values.name: keyboard
values.comment:
Turn your Text value into a styled button-looking block (in-line)

    text "CMD"
        |> keyboard
        |> toHtml

values.name: lineThrough
values.comment:
Set a striked line through your Text element

    text "forget about this"
        |> lineThrough True
        |> toHtml

values.name: listToHtml
values.comment: Render a list of Text elements into a span

values.name: strong
values.comment:
Set your Text element as bold

    text "look at me"
        |> strong
        |> toHtml

values.name: text
values.comment:
Create a text value

By default the text looks just like any regular text. To decorate it, you need to apply one or more of the below options.

text "hello, world!"
|> textType Warning
|> underlined True
|> strong
|> toHtml

values.name: textType
values.comment:
Change the text's type
text "Elm"
|> textType Secondary
|> toHtml

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Render your Text node into plain old `Html msg`

values.name: underlined
values.comment:
Add an underline to your text

    text "this is important"
        |> underlined True
        |> toHtml
